heading: Branch banks
label: 36 Branch banks

children.heading: Lawful and continuous operation
children.label: a Lawful and continuous operation
children.text: A national banking association may retain and operate such branch or branches as it may have had in lawful operation on , and any national banking association which continuously maintained and operated not more than one branch for a period of more than twenty-five years immediately preceding , may continue to maintain and operate such branch.February 25, 1927February 25, 1927

children.label: A
children.text: might be established under subsection (c) of this section as a new branch of the resulting national bank, and is approved by the Comptroller of the Currency for continued operation as a branch of the resulting national bank;

children.label: B
children.text: was a branch of any bank on ; orFebruary 25, 1927

children.label: C
children.text: is approved by the Comptroller of the Currency for continued operation as a branch of the resulting national bank.

children.label: A
children.text: a main office or branch office of any bank (other than the national bank) participating in the consolidation if, under subsection (c) of this section, it might be established as a new branch of the resulting bank, and if the Comptroller of the Currency approves of its continued operation after the consolidation;

children.label: B
children.text: a branch of any bank participating in the consolidation, and which, on , was in operation as a branch of any bank; orFebruary 25, 1927

children.label: C
children.text: a branch of the national bank and which, on , was not in operation as a branch of any bank, if the Comptroller of the Currency approves of its continued operation after the consolidation.February 25, 1927
As used in this subsection, the term “consolidation” includes a merger.

children.heading: New branches
children.label: c New branches
children.text: A national banking association may, with the approval of the Comptroller of the Currency, establish and operate new branches: (1) Within the limits of the city, town or village in which said association is situated, if such establishment and operation are at the time expressly authorized to State banks by the law of the State in question; and (2) at any point within the State in which said association is situated, if such establishment and operation are at the time authorized to State banks by the statute law of the State in question by language specifically granting such authority affirmatively and not merely by implication or recognition, and subject to the restrictions as to location imposed by the law of the State on State banks. In any State in which State banks are permitted by statute law to maintain branches within county or greater limits, if no bank is located and doing business in the place where the proposed agency is to be located, any national banking association situated in such State may, with the approval of the Comptroller of the Currency, establish and operate, without regard to the capital requirements of this section, a seasonal agency in any resort community within the limits of the county in which the main office of such association is located, for the purpose of receiving and paying out deposits, issuing and cashing checks and drafts, and doing business incident thereto: , That any permit issued under this sentence shall be revoked upon the opening of a State or national bank in such community. Except as provided in the immediately preceding sentence, no such association shall establish a branch outside of the city, town, or village in which it is situated unless it has a combined capital stock and surplus equal to the combined amount of capital stock and surplus, if any, required by the law of the State in which such association is situated for the establishment of such branches by State banks, or, if the law of such State requires only a minimum capital stock for the establishment of such branches by State banks, unless such association has not less than an equal amount of capital stock.Provided

children.heading: Branches resulting from interstate merger transactions
children.label: d Branches resulting from interstate merger transactions
children.text: A national bank resulting from an interstate merger transaction (as defined in section 1831u(f)(6) of this title) may maintain and operate a branch in a State other than the home State (as defined in subsection (g)(3)(B)) of such bank in accordance with .1section 1831u of this titleSee References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Exclusive authority for additional branches
children.label: e Exclusive authority for additional branches

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Effective , a national bank may not acquire, establish, or operate a branch in any State other than the bank’s home State (as defined in subsection (g)(3)(B)) or a State in which the bank already has a branch unless the acquisition, establishment, or operation of such branch in such State by such national bank is authorized under this section or section 1823(f), 1823(k), or 1831u of this title.June 1, 1997

children.label: A
children.text: the bank had no branches in such State; or

children.label: i
children.text: an interstate merger transaction approved pursuant to ; orsection 1831u of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a transaction after , pursuant to which the bank received assistance from the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation under .May 31, 1997section 1823(c) of this title

children.heading: Law applicable to interstate branching operations
children.label: f Law applicable to interstate branching operations

children.heading: Law applicable to national bank branches
children.label: 1 Law applicable to national bank branches
i
children.text: when Federal law preempts the application of such State laws to a national bank; or

children.label: ii
children.text: when the Comptroller of the Currency determines that the application of such State laws would have a discriminatory effect on the branch in comparison with the effect the application of such State laws would have with respect to branches of a bank chartered by the host State.

children.heading: Enforcement of applicable State laws
children.label: B Enforcement of applicable State laws
children.text: The provisions of any State law to which a branch of a national bank is subject under this paragraph shall be enforced, with respect to such branch, by the Comptroller of the Currency.

children.heading: Review and report on actions by Comptroller
children.label: C Review and report on actions by Comptroller
children.text: The Comptroller of the Currency shall conduct an annual review of the actions it has taken with regard to the applicability of State law to national banks (or their branches) during the preceding year, and shall include in its annual report required under the results of the review and the reasons for each such action. The first such review and report after , shall encompass all such actions taken on or after .section 14 of this titleJuly 3, 1997January 1, 1992

children.heading: Treatment of branch as bank
children.label: 2 Treatment of branch as bank
children.text: All laws of a host State, other than the laws regarding community reinvestment, consumer protection, fair lending, establishment of intrastate branches, and the application or administration of any tax or method of taxation, shall apply to a branch (in such State) of an out-of-State national bank to the same extent as such laws would apply if the branch were a national bank the main office of which is in such State.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 3 Rule of construction
children.text: No provision of this subsection may be construed as affecting the legal standards for preemption of the application of State law to national banks.

children.heading: State “opt-in” election to permit interstate branching through de novo branches
children.label: g State “opt-in” election to permit interstate branching through de novo branches
A
children.text: the law of the State in which the branch is located, or is to be located, would permit establishment of the branch, if the national bank were a State bank chartered by such State; and

children.label: B
children.text: the conditions established in, or made applicable to this paragraph by, paragraph (2) are met.

children.heading: Conditions on establishment and operation of interstate branch
children.label: 2 Conditions on establishment and operation of interstate branch

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: An application by a national bank to establish and operate a de novo branch in a host State shall be subject to the same requirements and conditions to which an application for an interstate merger transaction is subject under paragraphs (1), (3), and (4) of .section 1831u(b) of this title

children.heading: Operation
children.label: B Operation
children.text: Subsections (c) and (d)(2) of shall apply with respect to each branch of a national bank which is established and operated pursuant to an application approved under this subsection in the same manner and to the same extent such provisions of such apply to a branch of a national bank which resulted from an interstate merger transaction approved pursuant to such .section 1831u of this titlesection 1831u of this titlesection 1831u of this title

children.label: i
children.text: is originally established by the national bank as a branch; and

children.label: I
children.text: the acquisition by the bank of an insured depository institution or a branch of an insured depository institution; or

children.label: II
children.text: the conversion, merger, or consolidation of any such institution or branch.

children.heading: Home State
children.label: B Home State
children.text: The term “home State” means the State in which the main office of a national bank is located.

children.heading: Host State
children.label: C Host State
children.text: The term “host State” means, with respect to a bank, a State, other than the home State of the bank, in which the bank maintains, or seeks to establish and maintain, a branch.

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 104–208, div. A, title II, § 2204110 Stat. 3009–405Sept. 30, 1996
children.label: h Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 104–208, div. A, title II, § 2204110 Stat. 3009–405Sept. 30, 1996

children.heading: Prior approval of branch locations
children.label: i Prior approval of branch locations
children.text: No branch of any national banking association shall be established or moved from one location to another without first obtaining the consent and approval of the Comptroller of the Currency.

children.heading: “Branch” defined
children.label: j “Branch” defined
children.text: The term “branch” as used in this section shall be held to include any branch bank, branch office, branch agency, additional office, or any branch place of business located in any State or Territory of the United States or in the District of Columbia at which deposits are received, or checks paid, or money lent. The term “branch”, as used in this section, does not include an automated teller machine or a remote service unit.

children.heading: Branches in foreign countries, dependencies, or insular possessions
children.label: k Branches in foreign countries, dependencies, or insular possessions
children.text: This section shall not be construed to amend or repeal section 25 of the Federal Reserve Act, as amended [ et seq.], authorizing the establishment by national banking associations of branches in foreign countries, or dependencies, or insular possessions of the United States.12 U.S.C. 601

children.heading: “State bank” and “bank” defined
children.label: l “State bank” and “bank” defined
children.text: The words “State bank,” “State banks,” “bank,” or “banks,” as used in this section, shall be held to include trust companies, savings banks, or other such corporations or institutions carrying on the banking business under the authority of State laws.
